assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines available version as 'the newest version currently available in your GKE channel' and 'what you'd get if you manually upgraded right now'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — NOT necessarily the auto-upgrade target
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines default version as 'the version assigned to new clusters when no specific version is requested' and distinguishes it from auto-upgrade target

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines auto-upgrade target as 'the version your cluster will automatically upgrade to during the next maintenance window'

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response mentions the three versions can be different but doesn't specifically explain that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same or when they might differ during minor version rollouts

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions)
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance windows only in passing but doesn't explain how cluster constraints like maintenance exclusions affect the auto-upgrade target

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or how 'no minor' exclusions affect auto-upgrade behavior

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the cluster's auto-upgrade status to see the actual target version
assertions.reason: Response does not provide specific recommendation to check cluster auto-upgrade status, though it acknowledges the user already has this information

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE release schedule, release notes, or any specific resources for tracking version availability
